subject: Re: Exporting particles back to RELION after M refinement (MCore)
body:
Hi Alister,

Thanks for the quick response.

Then is there any way to transfer the RELION5 tomo format star file to
single particle format? I'd like to have the defocus and position
information for each tilt image of all particles.

Thanks
Yunjie

On Mon, Dec 30, 2024 at 1:34 PM Alister Burt <alisterburt@gmail.com> wrote:

> Hi Yunjie,
>
> This is not currently possible and support for exporting like this is not
> planned - if you have questions about how to interpret the RELION 5 format
> I’m happy to help.
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
> Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
>
> On Dec 29, 2024, at 21:22, Yunjie Chang <changyj0821@gmail.com> wrote:
>
> ﻿Hi Alister,
>
> I have a question about exporting particles by Warp. I checked the linked
> page in your previous reply to Juhua, but I want to do something different
> here.
>
> In the Windows M, there is an option to export particles after refinement
> and an option to choose export Volume or 2D particle stacks. If I choose
> the 2D particle stacks, M will generate the *.mrcs and the corresponding
> star file. This star file is in the RELION single particle format, with the
> defocus, position, Euler angles and other informations for each tilt image
> of every particle.
>
> However, in Linux Warp&M, if I use WarpTools ts_export_particles with --2d
> option, I still get the *.mrcs files for each particle, but the star file
> is in RELION5 tomo format. Is there any way to get the star file in RELION
> single particle format for Linux Warp&M?
>
> Thanks
> Yunjie
> On Tuesday, December 10, 2024 at 1:39:55 AM UTC+8 alist...@gmail.com
> wrote:
>
>> Hi Juha,
>>
>> c.f. https://github.com/warpem/warp/issues/149
>>
>> Cheers,
>>
>> Alister
>>
>> On Sat, 7 Dec 2024 at 01:24, Juha Huiskonen <juha.hu...@gmail.com> wrote:
>>
>>> Hi all,
>>>
>>> I have refined particles with MCore and I want to classify them further
>>> in RELION. What is the correct way to do this?
>>>
>>> First, I tried using the particles.star file from
>>> M/species/particle/particles.star in WarpTools ts_export_particles. This
>>> STAR file is, however, not compatible.
>>>
>>> Should I use the STAR file created by RELION (the same one I used to
>>> create the species in M) instead? Does this workflow use the updated XML
>>> files for tilt series alignment? Are the updated particle alignment
>>> parameters in the M/species/particle/particles.star file relevant here?
>>>
>>> Cheers,
>>> Juha
>>>
>>> --
>>> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google
>>> Groups "Warp" group.
>>> To unsubscribe from this group and stop receiving emails from it, send
>>> an email to warp-em+u...@googlegroups.com.
>>> To view this discussion visit
>>> https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/c43a9274-ecb1-4654-9d57-e5a11653069cn%40googlegroups.com
>>> <https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/c43a9274-ecb1-4654-9d57-e5a11653069cn%40googlegroups.com?utm_medium=email&utm_source=footer>
>>> .
>>>
>> --

> .
>
>

-- 

Best
Yunjie Chang
